channel.title: Report on My Cybersecurity Portfolio
channel.description: Recent content in Report on My Cybersecurity Portfolio

item.title: SECUREOPS Penetration Test Report
item.description:
Penetration Test Report - Legacy Development Environment

Target: Metasploitable 3 (Ubuntu 14.04) – 10.0.2.13 Assessment Date: May 31, 2026 Prepared For: SECUREOPS Internal Security Team Prepared By: Titus Kamau – Junior Penetration Tester

Executive Summary

A penetration test was conducted against the legacy development environment to evaluate its security posture.

CRITICAL FINDING: An unauthenticated attacker can achieve full root access within minutes using public exploits.

Key Vulnerabilities Identified

Vulnerability Severity CVSS
Drupalgeddon (CVE-2014-3704) Critical 9.8
UnrealIRCd Backdoor Critical 9.8
ProFTPD mod_copy RCE (CVE-2015-3306) Critical 9.8
SambaCry (CVE-2017-7494) Critical 9.8
PwnKit (CVE-2021-4034) High 7.8

Attack Path Summary

  1. Reconnaissance: Nmap scan revealed open ports 21,22,80,445,6697,8080
  2. Initial Access: Drupalgeddon exploit provided Meterpreter shell as www-data
  3. Privilege Escalation: PwnKit one-liner escalated to root access
  4. Post-Exploitation: Captured multiple flag files proving full compromise

Remediation Priorities

Priority Action
Critical Patch Drupal, remove UnrealIRCd, upgrade ProFTPD/Samba
High Update polkit (PwnKit), remove backdoor user account
Medium Upgrade Apache, implement proper logging

Skills Demonstrated

  • Vulnerability identification and exploitation
  • Privilege escalation techniques
  • Professional report writing
  • Remediation recommendation

View Full Report
